title: Crunchy Bhuna Cabbage Stir-fry
description: A rich, crunchy, meal-prep Ethiopian dish featuring cabbage leaves with a mediterranean-friendly bhuna sauce.

ingredients.name: Cabbage
ingredients.notes: Use the outer leaves for added crunch

ingredients.name: Tomato
ingredients.notes: Juiced and chopped into small pieces

ingredients.name: Turmeric Powder
ingredients.notes: Optional for added flavor and color

ingredients.name: Oil
ingredients.notes: Any neutral oil can be used, such as sunflower or vegetable

instructions: Wash and cut the cabbage into large leaves. Rinse in cold water and squeeze out excess moisture.
Heat oil in a pan over medium-high heat. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter for about 15 seconds, then add onion and garlic. Sauté until onions are translucent.
Add tomatoes, green peppers, red chillies, turmeric powder (if using), coriander powder, and salt to the pan. Mix well and cook for another minute.
Arrange cabbage leaves in a non-stick wok or large skillet. Pour the tomato mixture over them evenly. Cover with a lid if available, otherwise let it sit without covering for 5 minutes.
Gently flip the cabbage to coat all sides and ensure even cooking. If using a wok, move the ingredients around frequently to prevent burning.
Once all sides of the cabbage are cooked to your liking, serve hot.
